jokes.text: God! Sorry! Sorry! What's happening? I can't hear! And I'm dying!
jokes.setup: Ben tries to surprise Leslie with something involving cake
jokes.punchline: Leslie's over-the-top dramatic reaction to what's clearly a minor surprise
jokes.explanation: Standard physical overreaction bit - functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: Will you go to prom with me? Well, this just keeps getting weirder.
jokes.setup: Cake message asks Leslie to prom
jokes.punchline: Ben's dry commentary on the escalating absurdity
jokes.explanation: Good use of Ben's deadpan character voice to comment on Leslie's intensity

jokes.text: Which reminds me, I need to learn Latin.
jokes.setup: Leslie volunteers to teach A.P. Latin to help the school
jokes.punchline: Casual admission she doesn't actually know Latin yet
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment - overly ambitious with casual dismissal of practical concerns

jokes.text: Legend has it that he can still turn on a microwave just by blinking at it.
jokes.setup: Harvey nearly died from VCR electrocution
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous superhero-like side effect from the accident
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation that turns a throwaway detail into something memorable

jokes.text: Well, I thought you'd never ask, because we're nearing 40.
jokes.setup: Leslie formally asks Ben to prom
jokes.punchline: Ben points out the absurdity of their age vs. the prom invitation
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about the age inappropriate nature of their prom date

jokes.text: Aw, did you want to DJ, little puppy? I didn't know that little puppies could operate an iPod with their little puppy paws.
jokes.setup: Tom gets assigned DJ duties over Ben
jokes.punchline: Tom's condescending baby talk taken to absurd extreme
jokes.explanation: Good character work for Tom but the execution is a bit overdone

jokes.text: Eew and boo. This kind of thing is getting out of hand with you two.
jokes.setup: Tom and Ben's childish bickering
jokes.punchline: April's disgusted commentary delivered in her signature flat tone
jokes.explanation: Perfect April voice - economical and cutting

jokes.text: I had a show called 'Zoot Suit Wyatt' on Carleton College radio. Tuesdays from 3:00 to 5:00 A.M., I was the king of swing.
jokes.setup: Ben reveals he used to DJ
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous name and terrible time slot reveal his past delusions of grandeur
jokes.explanation: Great character building - reveals Ben's nerdy past with specific embarrassing details

jokes.text: I thought we as a culture agreed to forget the year that everyone was into swing.
jokes.setup: Ben's swing music past
jokes.punchline: Tom's broader cultural commentary on the swing revival
jokes.explanation: Smart pop culture reference that feels earned rather than forced

jokes.text: Are those jense-trodder color tabs? I thought those were discontinued. They were. I had to order them through some Mexican back channels. Juan Julio Oficina Supplies?
jokes.setup: Leslie notices Allison's binder organization
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific office supply knowledge escalating to international procurement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy showing Leslie finding her organizational soulmate

jokes.text: What is happening right now?
jokes.setup: Leslie and Allison's intense office supply bonding
jokes.punchline: Ben's confused outsider perspective
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that grounds the absurdity

jokes.text: Every song I download has to pass a series of rigorous tests to answer one simple question: Is it a banger?
jokes.setup: Tom explains his music curation philosophy
jokes.punchline: Overly scientific approach to determining if a song 'bangs'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom character moment - treating something trivial with excessive seriousness

jokes.text: I once accidentally downloaded a Lumineers song. I had to throw away my whole computer just to be safe.
jokes.setup: Tom's strict 'banger' requirements
jokes.punchline: Extreme overreaction to accidentally downloading acoustic music
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that perfectly captures Tom's music snobbery

jokes.text: I hope that high school auditorium's big enough, 'cause I'm bringing 10,000 maniacs.
jokes.setup: Ben talks about DJ set list flow
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun on the band name '10,000 Maniacs'
jokes.explanation: Dad joke level pun that fits Ben's dorky character but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: Sometimes it's really hard to be friends with you.
jokes.setup: Ben's terrible music pun
jokes.punchline: Tom's deadpan response to Ben's corniness
jokes.explanation: Good reaction that acknowledges the bad pun

jokes.text: DJ Robo Drop. ♪ Ba-da-da, mm, mm, mm, mm-mm ♪
jokes.setup: Tom declares he's working alone now
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous DJ name with accompanying sound effects
jokes.explanation: Typical Tom over-the-top persona creation

jokes.text: Or, no, we can't, because they moved to prison.
jokes.setup: Leslie fondly remembers parents who let them drink at after-parties
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that they're now in prison
jokes.explanation: Good dark turn that subverts the nostalgic setup

jokes.text: Prom is nothing but a huge party full of smiling, dancing people enjoying themselves. It's literally my worst nightmare.
jokes.setup: April describes what prom is
jokes.punchline: Her perfectly April-like conclusion that fun = nightmare
jokes.explanation: Pure April character moment that sets up her prom arc

jokes.text: And I hate punch.
jokes.setup: April listing why prom is her nightmare
jokes.punchline: Additional petty complaint about punch
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that adds to April's anti-prom stance

jokes.text: ♪ Sarah Wei--April Ludgate ♪
jokes.setup: Andy sings romantic song he wrote years ago
jokes.punchline: Has to awkwardly change the name mid-lyric
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment that shows Andy's romantic but impractical nature

jokes.text: Fine. I'll go with you, because Leslie's making us, and we live together, and we only have one car right now because you laked mine.
jokes.setup: Andy asks April to prom
jokes.punchline: April's reluctant acceptance with the invented verb 'laked'
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay that hints at Andy's destructive tendencies

jokes.text: What does 'laked' mean? I tried to jump it over a lake.
jokes.setup: Andy asks what 'laked' means
jokes.punchline: April's matter-of-fact explanation of Andy's ridiculous car stunt
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that perfectly captures both characters - Andy's impulsiveness and April's deadpan delivery

jokes.text: We here at the parks department have something called 'The April Ludgate Summer Solstice Druid Festival and Buffalo Wings Eating Contest.' I don't know why I let her name it.
jokes.setup: Leslie explains the internship program
jokes.punchline: The absurdly long, April-influenced name and Leslie's regret
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Leslie's tendency to let April run wild with naming

jokes.text: Is there some secret society for the greatest humans on earth? Do you meet Tom Hanks and Elena Kagan in the Statue of Liberty's crown?
jokes.setup: Leslie discovers Ron and Allison know each other
jokes.punchline: Leslie's increasingly specific conspiracy theory about excellence
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie escalation with specific pop culture references

jokes.text: God, this fake club I'm inventing is amazing.
jokes.setup: Leslie's elaborate conspiracy theory
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on her own imagination
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment where Leslie acknowledges her own absurdity

jokes.text: What brings you to the festering putrid stink hole on the armpit of freedom? That's what he calls city hall.
jokes.setup: Ron greets Allison at city hall
jokes.punchline: His characteristically extreme anti-government description
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron character moment with his signature anti-government hyperbole

jokes.text: Why don't you shut your mustache?
jokes.setup: Ron lectures Allison about government work
jokes.punchline: Allison's creative insult targeting Ron's signature feature
jokes.explanation: Creative insult that shows Allison can hold her own against Ron

jokes.text: Oh, really? Is it worth a few bucks to get a million splinters in your fingers and then cut off your fingers accidentally, 'cause that will happen.
jokes.setup: Allison expresses interest in the sawmill job
jokes.punchline: Leslie's increasingly dire warnings about sawmill dangers
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Leslie's desperation but a bit overdone

jokes.text: I once did him a favor. I built his sawmill.
jokes.setup: Ron mentions doing Tim Hautner a favor
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals he built the entire sawmill
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - understatement of a massive construction project delivered deadpan

jokes.text: And if Ron tries to stop me, he can eat a big ol' bowl of butts.
jokes.setup: Leslie's determination to recruit Allison
jokes.punchline: Childish but emphatic threat against Ron
jokes.explanation: Juvenile insult that fits Leslie's competitive spirit but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: If you don't show up in a limo, legally, they can't let you in.
jokes.setup: April questions why Andy paid for a limo
jokes.punchline: Andy's completely false but confidently stated 'legal' requirement
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his tendency to state nonsense with complete confidence

jokes.text: Who's Orin? I'm April's mom.
jokes.setup: Andy calls out to someone named Orin
jokes.punchline: It's actually April's mom, showing April's goth influence on family
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag setup suggesting April's mom looks like a goth character

jokes.text: Where's my Yeezus Mountain?
jokes.setup: Tom complains about missing equipment
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous request for a Kanye-themed mountain structure
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Tom request that shows his over-the-top expectations

jokes.text: How am I supposed to keep my Husker Du albums in near-mint condish?
jokes.setup: Ben complains about missing elliptical cartridges
jokes.punchline: Reveals his obsessive vinyl collecting with cringey abbreviated slang
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ben's nerdy obsessions and awkward slang

jokes.text: Hey, I have an idea. Why don't you throw them in the garbage?
jokes.setup: Ben's precious vinyl concerns
jokes.punchline: Tom's dismissive suggestion to trash them
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic response that works for Tom's character

jokes.text: By the time I got my Armani suit pressed and got the little dimple on my tie just right, it was 11:45 P.M.
jokes.setup: Tom explains why he was only at prom for 15 minutes
jokes.punchline: His obsessive grooming routine made him arrive at the very end
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom character moment showing his vanity over practicality

jokes.text: My date was pretty pissed, but, uh, I looked fly as hell.
jokes.setup: Tom's late arrival to prom
jokes.punchline: His complete lack of concern for his date's feelings
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that shows Tom's self-centered priorities

jokes.text: Oh, did he? I didn't even notice. No bigs.
jokes.setup: Ben mentions the principal gave Leslie an A-plus
jokes.punchline: Leslie's transparent attempt to seem casual about grades
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Leslie can't hide her grade obsession

jokes.text: I attended prom with Susan Hofler. Picked her up in my truck, we slow-danced to a Merle Haggard song, and then I left early to go to my shift at the quarry. I was 12 years old.
jokes.setup: Ron describes his prom experience matter-of-factly
jokes.punchline: Shocking revelation that he was only 12
jokes.explanation: Masterful escalation that recontextualizes everything - perfect Ron backstory that's both absurd and believable for his character

jokes.text: Like flowers on the sunset of an eagle's poetry.
jokes.setup: Andy describes the beauty of youth at prom
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical but earnest metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Andy line that sounds profound but means nothing

jokes.text: It's like the movie Expendables 2. First time, hated it. Second time, hated it. Third time, it was okay. But then the fourth, fifth, sixth, seventh, tenth time I watched it, I realized something. It's just--it's not good. It's not a good movie.
jokes.setup: Andy compares prom to a movie experience, building expectation he'll say he loved it
jokes.punchline: After watching it ten times, concludes it's still bad
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that subverts expectations perfectly - great structure

jokes.text: Um, I'm gonna go to the girls' bathroom to check the trash cans for babies, but you can dance if you want.
jokes.setup: April agrees to let Andy dance
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions checking for abandoned babies
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark April line delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: The stuff you're playing is so old, it's probably trinking on grizzledump. Hey, what's grizzledump? And why is trinking on it bad?
jokes.setup: Teen dismisses Ben's music with made-up slang
jokes.punchline: Ben's desperate attempt to understand the meaningless terms
jokes.explanation: Good generational humor showing Ben's crisis about being out of touch

jokes.text: What's grizzledump, Ben? What's grizzledump?
jokes.setup: Ben's confusion about teen slang
jokes.punchline: Tom's mocking repetition of Ben's desperation
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that amplifies Ben's panic

jokes.text: Congratulations, Ron. You've ruined prom. Everybody's having a terrible time.
jokes.setup: Ron and Leslie's confusing instructions to Allison
jokes.punchline: Leslie blames Ron for the confusion while everyone is clearly having fun
jokes.explanation: Good ironic overreaction from Leslie

jokes.text: I was dating an older fella back then. Like I'm gonna dance with a bunch of kids while he's coaching Duke to the final four.
jokes.setup: Donna explains why she didn't go to her own prom
jokes.punchline: Reveals she was dating Coach K during his championship run
jokes.explanation: Great Donna moment with specific sports reference that fits her character

jokes.text: How can I love someone when I hate everything he loves? That's not true. He loves a lot of dumb stuff, but he loves you the most.
jokes.setup: April's relationship crisis about incompatibility
jokes.punchline: Donna's wise but blunt relationship advice
jokes.explanation: Good relationship humor that's both funny and touching

jokes.text: I just spoke with Tim Hautner from the sawmill on my mobile telephone, and he's offering Allison a paying job.
jokes.setup: Ron announces he got Allison a job
jokes.punchline: His formal reference to his 'mobile telephone' suggests an old/large phone
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ron's old-fashioned technology preferences

jokes.text: First of all, your telephone is ridiculous.
jokes.setup: Ron mentions his mobile telephone
jokes.punchline: Leslie's immediate mockery of whatever phone Ron has
jokes.explanation: Good reaction that confirms the visual gag about Ron's phone

jokes.text: I wouldn't say 'harassing' so much as persistently tormenting.
jokes.setup: Principal accuses them of harassing Allison
jokes.punchline: Leslie's attempt to use a 'better' word that's actually worse
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that shows Leslie's inability to admit wrongdoing

jokes.text: I think the only thing that matters is whatever A-pluses may or may not have been handed out, you know, are still in effect in perpetuity.
jokes.setup: Leslie agrees to stop bothering Allison
jokes.punchline: Immediately tries to negotiate keeping her grade
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Leslie can't let go of academic validation

jokes.text: You're gonna take this internship. You're gonna fall in love with public service. You're gonna rise through the ranks. You'll take over Ron's job as parks director. You'll win a seat in congress, and then you and I will run against each other as president, but right before we find out who won, I'm gonna pat you on the back and say, 'It's your turn, kid.'
jokes.setup: Leslie explains Allison's future
jokes.punchline: Increasingly grandiose predictions ending in presidential campaign
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that perfectly captures Leslie's political ambitions and mentorship fantasies

jokes.text: And if this is the evening you decide to have sex, use protection, please!
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to dismiss the kids normally
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sex ed advice at prom
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Leslie's inability to stop giving advice

jokes.text: Sorry. Tried to use slang. That just came out.
jokes.setup: Ben calls the kids 'snoopies'
jokes.punchline: His immediate embarrassed explanation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ben's desperate attempt to be cool

jokes.text: Where'd you get that dress? I was buried in it.
jokes.setup: Student asks about April's dress
jokes.punchline: April's morbid but matter-of-fact response
jokes.explanation: Perfect April line - dark, economical, and perfectly delivered. Classic quotable moment

jokes.text: Because this prom reminds me that... you had, like, a million friends in high school and everyone thought you were awesome, and I spent four years making fun of everyone and hiding mops so the janitor would think he was going insane.
jokes.setup: April explains why she's not having fun
jokes.punchline: Reveals her cruel but creative high school pranks
jokes.explanation: Great character backstory that's both mean and oddly creative

jokes.text: I have never told you this, but I actually have a ten-point scale for how insane you are being. I observe your behavior, and if it's a five or below, I say nothing.
jokes.setup: Ron addresses Leslie's behavior
jokes.punchline: Reveals he has a scientific system for monitoring her craziness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment that explains so much about their relationship

jokes.text: Smart. Saves a lot of time.
jokes.setup: Ron explains his system for dealing with her
jokes.punchline: Leslie's matter-of-fact acceptance of being monitored for insanity
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing Leslie's self-awareness about her intensity

jokes.text: They wrote all the team names in pink. Nobody told them to do that.
jokes.setup: Ron mentions a level 6 incident with soccer uniforms
jokes.punchline: Leslie's defensive explanation of her outrage over uniform colors
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie's control issues over trivial details

jokes.text: Blueprints for the future are a fool's errand. They're like blueprints for a house. Nice to have, but any foreman with half a brain doesn't need to look at them.
jokes.setup: Leslie worries about leaving Pawnee
jokes.punchline: Ron's analogy comparing life plans to construction blueprints
jokes.explanation: Good Ron wisdom with construction metaphor that fits his character

jokes.text: Damn it. I just wish once you would say something stupid so I could ignore it.
jokes.setup: Ron gives unexpectedly good advice
jokes.punchline: Leslie's frustrated reaction to Ron being wise
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie's appreciation for Ron despite herself

jokes.text: What? I broke the box, and I threw away all the votes, and I voted for you a thousand times.
jokes.setup: Andy wins prom king
jokes.punchline: April's matter-of-fact confession of election fraud
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - romantic but in the most destructive way possible

jokes.text: He's 33, and I'm 47/immortal.
jokes.setup: Student asks how old they are
jokes.punchline: April's deadpan delivery of impossible age
jokes.explanation: Perfect April line that's both absurd and believable from her

jokes.text: High school forever!
jokes.setup: Andy and April get kicked out of prom
jokes.punchline: Andy's enthusiastic embrace of the teen experience
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andy's eternal optimism

jokes.text: I rigged an election and got kicked out of prom. I can get on board with that.
jokes.setup: Andy says April is having fun now
jokes.punchline: April's approval based on the destructive/rebellious elements
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing April only enjoys chaos and rule-breaking

jokes.text: My life is over. I'm officially old and uncool. Might as well start wearing a plaid shirt and a tie and khakis and whatever shoes you're wearing.
jokes.setup: Ben's crisis about being out of touch
jokes.punchline: Describes exactly what he's already wearing as old person clothes
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment about Ben's nerdy style

jokes.text: How else do you explain their terrible haircuts?
jokes.setup: Tom comforts Ben about successful people being uncool
jokes.punchline: Ben's theory about billionaire haircuts
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about celebrity style choices

jokes.text: Yeah. You're old as shit.
jokes.setup: Tom says Ben will never be as old as him
jokes.punchline: Ben's blunt agreement that turns the comfort into an insult
jokes.explanation: Good reversal that subverts Tom's attempt at comfort

jokes.text: Get her away from me, or I'm gonna kidnap her and try to mentor her to death.
jokes.setup: Leslie admires Allison's thoughtfulness
jokes.punchline: Leslie's threat to over-mentor Allison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie line showing her mentorship obsession taken to extreme

jokes.text: Greg Pikitis. What up, Knope?
jokes.setup: Allison introduces her boyfriend
jokes.punchline: It's Leslie's teenage nemesis from previous episodes
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that recontextualizes the entire Allison storyline

jokes.text: Offer is off the table. She's a terrible person with terrible judgment.
jokes.setup: Discovery that Allison is dating Greg Pikitis
jokes.punchline: Leslie's immediate reversal of her opinion of Allison
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Leslie's instant judgment reversal based on association
